summary: Iteration 2 — tested the sequential refactor (Research→Strategy→Content; phase_a/phase_b removed). Backend pytest suite was rewritten for the new contract: 25/25 passed (health=gtm_sequential, deprecated endpoints 404/405, create-run starts at research, all 3 PDF scopes + 3 DOCX scopes + PPTX deck + ZIP exports produce the spec'd filenames and sizes, ZIP archive contains pdf/ docx/ pptx/ subdirs, file download works for root + subdir files, bad format/scope return 4xx, strategy export pre-strategy returns 400, chat works for research/strategy/content scopes, negative cases pass). Used existing seeded complete run 7da57a83 (has report+gtm_strategy+content) to avoid a 5-min pipeline run. Frontend: landing hero/MOVE logo render; /studio empty state OK; /studio with seeded run renders the GTM-kit ZIP banner, all 3 tabs, all 8 export testids (export-pdf-research/strategy/combined, export-docx-research/strategy/combined, export-pptx, export-zip), and an export click produced the correct toast ('research_report_openai.pdf ready'). HOWEVER, the 'Ask the agent' tab crashes with an Uncaught TypeError because ChatPanel was not updated for the new scope='content' — see Action Items.

minor.issue: Bad format/scope returns 422 (Pydantic Literal) rather than 400. Spec says 400. Either is acceptable but if strict 400 is desired wrap with custom validator.

ui_bugs.issue: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map') when ContentStudio's 'Ask the agent' tab mounts <ChatPanel scope="content" />. Root cause: QUICK and TITLE dicts in /app/frontend/src/components/ChatPanel.jsx only define keys 'research' and 'strategy'; 'content' is missing, so `QUICK[scope].map(...)` on line 91 crashes. Effect: the entire chat tab does not render — no chat-panel-content/chat-input-content/chat-send-content elements exist in the DOM, and a CRA error overlay covers the page. Fix: add `content: [...]` to QUICK and `content: 'Ask about the content'` to TITLE (or guard with `(QUICK[scope] || []).map`). Intro message also hard-codes research/strategy only.

approve-content / regenerate-content buttons only render when run.status === 'awaiting_content_approval'. The seeded run is already 'complete', so these testids cannot be observed in a completed state. This is the correct intent — flagging for awareness; main agent may want a smoke run reaching awaiting_content_approval to verify these visually.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot be exercised end-to-end on /studio because the panel crashes before mount. Backend /api/runs/{id}/chat with scope='content' works (verified in pytest), so this is purely a frontend regression.

action_items: FIX (HIGH): /app/frontend/src/components/ChatPanel.jsx — add 'content' entries to the QUICK and TITLE dictionaries (and the intro-message ternary on line 30) so scope='content' renders without crashing. Without this, the entire 'Ask the agent' tab is broken on /studio.
Optional: convert export validation errors from 422 to 400 if strict spec compliance is desired.
critical_code_review_comments: ChatPanel.jsx wasn't updated alongside the refactor that introduced the 'content' chat scope — exact symptom of an incomplete rename. Recommend a smoke unit test or PropTypes/TS guard on `scope`.

web_orchestrator.export() relies on `result.report` being present even for scope='strategy' (see _scoped_result). That's fine for current pipelines but tightly couples strategy export to a research artifact; consider making the strategy export self-contained.

/api/runs/{id}/files/{filename} silently searches first-level subdirs by basename — collisions across pdf/docx/pptx folders are theoretically possible since the file extensions differ, so OK in practice, but worth documenting.
Deprecated route surface returns 405 (Method Not Allowed) rather than 404 for some paths. Consider explicitly returning 404 for removed routes so clients fail loudly.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — landing/studio/exports/tabs all OK; 'Ask the agent' tab broken by ChatPanel bug

test_credentials: N/A — app has no user auth
seed_data_creation: Reused existing complete run 7da57a83-626e-47b2-9289-044d2e4fbd6c (has result.report + result.gtm_strategy + result.content) for export and chat tests. Created and deleted a transient TEST_seq_smoke run and a TEST_no_strategy_yet run as part of the pytest run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sequential pipeline is solid: pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers the new contract and runs in <30s by reusing the seeded run. The single open frontend bug is ChatPanel.jsx missing the 'content' scope in its QUICK/TITLE dicts (crashes the Ask-the-agent tab). After main agent fixes ChatPanel, retest just the /studio chat tab. To test a live pipeline reaching awaiting_content_approval (so approve-content / regenerate-content testids appear), expect ~3-5 min runtime.
rca of the issue: ChatPanel was refactored to accept scope='content' (used by ContentStudio's new 'Ask the agent' tab), but the QUICK and TITLE constants inside ChatPanel.jsx still only contain entries for 'research' and 'strategy'. When ContentStudio mounts <ChatPanel scope='content' />, the ChatPanel becomes 'ready' (because run.status === 'complete') and tries to render quick prompts via `QUICK[scope].map(...)`. Since `QUICK['content']` is undefined, this throws TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'map'), unmounting the entire tab tree and showing the CRA error overlay. Reproduction: localStorage.setItem('move:active_run_id', '7da57a83-...'), navigate to /studio, click [data-testid='tab-chat']. Mitigation: in /app/frontend/src/components/ChatPanel.jsx add `content: ['What is the headline insight?', 'Draft a LinkedIn post for this', 'Summarise the email sequence']` (or similar) to QUICK and `content: 'Ask about the content'` to TITLE; update the intro-message ternary on line 30 to handle 'content'.
